description: PLMKR Fan and social knowledge base — load order for knowledge assembly

files.description: Specialist identity and mission (depth of fan relationship and sustainable superfan revenue concentration), the foundational mandate (own the relationship not the algorithm) with the revenue-concentration and parasocial-responsibility principles, philosophy, decision style and biases, the six-part judgment doctrine (owned-vs-rented channel priority, superfan revenue-concentration rules, monetization-timing with the enumeration rule, platform-investment triage with the NOT EVALUABLE pattern, engagement quality over vanity metrics, fan-data ethics lines), hard refusals, and communication style

Named frameworks (participatory culture, parasocial interaction, identity fusion, affective economics, sense of community, the five-stage fan engagement spectrum), decision branching for when attachment deepens or erodes and depth-vs-breadth, domain anti-patterns, and music modules (genre fan-culture typology, the parasocial-rupture recovery framework by severity, fan identity in the AI era)

Owned vs. rented community media and migration economics, the hub-and-spoke model with hub selection, the community lifecycle model, social capital (bonding/bridging), community health indicators, the moderation framework and crisis decision tree, domain anti-patterns, and music modules (community-server architecture, SMS/email, the platform-migration pattern, membership-hub model, listening-party architecture)

The true-fans thesis, the revenue-concentration principle, the fan LTV framework, three-tier membership design with the paywall-sequencing rule, D2C commerce margins, decision branching for membership launch and fan-funding vs. pre-order vs. subscription, domain anti-patterns, and music modules (platform-specific monetization, fan-club tier economics, fan-funding and crowdfunding)

The three-type algorithm taxonomy (interest/social/intent graph), the content-format taxonomy, the sound-discovery flywheel, domain anti-patterns, and music modules covering short-video, social-feed, video, text/discourse, live-streaming, community-server, and regional platforms with the platform-native content principle and currency-verification discipline

Fan identity resolution, behavioral segmentation, the adapted RFM+Depth+Advocacy model, first-party data priority, the analytics hierarchy, decision branching for CRM investment and stage-based data collection, pre-save as data capture, domain anti-patterns, and music modules (DSP-for-artists mining with save rate, email-list building, tooling categories)

The six-stage fan journey map, activation-moment theory, the retention/churn framework with the inactivity curve, reactivation triggers, lifecycle-stage thresholds, decision branching for acquisition vs. retention and reactivation ROI, domain anti-patterns, and music modules (album-cycle journey, concert as conversion event, engineered lifecycles, artist-inactivity management)

Artist-voice vs. brand-voice, the content-authenticity framework (spontaneity, emotional honesty, specificity), the UGC cultivation model, the planned-vs-reactive calendar, the behind-the-scenes content hierarchy, decision branching, domain anti-patterns, and music modules (fan-channel content types ranked, the teaser-and-reveal model, fan-participation mechanics)

The five-tier ambassador taxonomy, incentivization structure with the anti-mercenary rule, street-team management, the fan-labor ethics framework with the coercion test, digital advocacy mechanics, decision branching for program launch and incentive selection, domain anti-patterns, and music modules (organized-fandom mobilization, street-team history, campus ambassador programs)

The eight-dimension Fanbase Health model (Community Health, Superfan Concentration, D2C Conversion, Engagement Lifecycle, Platform Presence, Content Quality, Fan Data Maturity, Ambassador Program) with weights, the MEASURED/SOURCED/JUDGED evidence discipline and NOT EVALUABLE rule, letter-to-numeric mapping, dimension anchors, the four hard gates, the mandatory owned-channel test, and the verdict ladder

Output discipline (mandatory core, decision-type fields, conditional modules), the module table, the four templates (fanbase health evaluation, fan strategy recommendation, platform investment decision, community crisis response with the financially-affected-fans protocol), handoff boundaries to other domains, and hard refusals

The live-conversion stack (festival → support → headline → VIP scaling by depth); presale architecture with tier sequencing rule (fan-club before platform presale); VIP package design taxonomy with the photo-op trap and the intimacy compression principle; virtual concert mechanics and pricing; post-show 72-hour engagement window protocol; venue fan-experience design (queue, merch table, setlist, fan sections); tour merchandise commission structure and D2C integration; festival vs. headline show strategy distinction; listening-party production mechanics; multi-market on-sale sequencing

Four core automation sequences (welcome, pre-release, post-purchase, win-back) with the 72-hour trigger rule; welcome sequence structure (5-email, day-by-day); email deliverability mechanics (sender reputation, complaint rate thresholds, list hygiene, domain authentication — SPF/DKIM/DMARC); SMS compliance framework by jurisdiction (TCPA, GDPR, ePrivacy, A2P 10DLC, short codes, double opt-in); SMS character-limit and Unicode-encoding mechanics; frequency caps; six-tier behavioral list segmentation with segment-first deployment protocol; release-cycle email sequence; SMS cadence for tour on-sale; email A/B testing sample-size framework; list growth capture-touchpoint ranking; the 90-day engagement cliff practitioner insight

notes.entity_wall: These files are PLMKR-owned originals. Do not reference external sources, rubric identifiers, sibling products, or entity names from any source material.
notes.mock_mode: Deterministic knowledge assembly only — no live LLM/API calls during loading or testing.
